coredata.dc:title: Effect of gamma irradiation on carbon dot decorated polyethylene-gold@ hydroxyapatite biocomposite on titanium implanted repair for shoulder joint arthroplasty
coredata.prism:publicationName: Journal of Photochemistry and Photobiology B: Biology
coredata.dc:creator: Lu, Ke
Li, Cong
Wang, Hong-zhen
Li, Yun-long
Zhu, Yi
Ouyang, Yuanming
coredata.dc:description: High disappointment rate of the ligament to hard tissue mending after the medical procedure has dependably been a testing issue in rotator cuff repair. Considering the elasticity of carbon dot decorated polyethylene (f-CDs-PE) and osteogenic movement of gold substituted hydroxyapatite (Au@HA) bioceramic, f-CDs-PE-Au@HA biocomposite coatings were created by an electrophoretic deposition method (EPD), the in vivo and in vitro bioactivity and cytocompatibility were researched. The physico-chemical properties of f-CDs-PE-Au@HA biocomposite coatings were characterized using fourier transform infra-red (FTIR) and X-Ray diffractometery (XRD). The morphology of the fabricated biocomposites was analyses via scanning electron microscopy (SEM) and transmission electron microscopy (TEM) methods. With a gamma-irradiation of f-CDs-PE-Au@HA biocomposite coating (BC2), the bond and multiplication of cells on biocomposite coating were improved. The specimen with a f-CDs-PE-Au@HA biocomposite (BC2) demonstrated a most noteworthy alkaline phosphatase activity articulation. The animal model consequences additionally show that the f-CDs-PE-Au@HA biocomposite (BC2) had great bioactive and cytocompatibility, which could develop the association of collagen and the arrangement of ligament and hard tissue. Expansion of the gamma-ray irradiation with f-CDs-PE-Au@HA biocomposite coating (BC2) at the tendon- hard tissue crossing point was exhibited to reinforce the mending entheses, increment hard tissue and tendon development and progress collagen association contrasted and control. The above outcomes have recommended that the progressive, implantable and solid stringy platforms built utilizing EPD extraordinary potential for enlargement of rotator cuff tears-recuperating.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Biocomposite coating
Electrophoretic deposition technique
Tendon to bone healing
Rotator cuff tear
Shoulder

xocs:meta.xocs:srctitle: Journal of Photochemistry and Photobiology B: Biology

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Fabrication of f-CDs, Au@HA, and f-CDs-PE-Au@HA Composite

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: The structural formation and phase purity were evaluated through (A) FT-IR spectra and (B) XRD spectra of prepared biocomposite (BC1 and BC2) in the presence and absence of γ- irradiation.

SEM and cross-section images of (A, B) pure f-CDs-PE-Au@HA biocomposite coating; (B, D) gamma-ray treated f-CDs-PE-Au@HA biocomposite coating and micro-scratch analysis of biocomposites coated Ti implants with and without irradiation of γ-radiation (LC; Critical load failures).
TEM/SAD image of (A, A1) f-CDs; (B, B1) pure f-CDs-PE-Au@HA biocomposite coating; (C, C1) gamma-ray treated f-CDs-PE-Au@HA biocomposite coating.
In vitro osteoblast cell compatibility analysis (A), MTT assay for measuring cell viability (B), and ECM activity (C) treated with biocomposites (BC1 and BC2); (scale bar = 50 μm).

ALP activity of synthesized biocomposites (BC1 and BC2) on the osteoblast cell culture (A; staining images & B; quantitative analysis) and analogous collagen quantitative outcomes (C) (scale bar = 50 μm).

(A) Postoperative radiograph demonstrating the activity of prepared composite coatings with intact and stable anatomic total shoulder arthroplasty and the quantitative measurement of the calculated bone mineral density (B) with bone volume fraction (C).

Representative H&E stained tissue sections (A) and picrosirius red–stained tissue sections of the healing enthesis (B) for the control, biocomposites with and without irradiation of gamma radiation (BC1 and BC2) after 14 and 28 days, postoperatively and quantitative analysis of collagen birefringence (C) treated with prepared samples (scale bar = 20 μm). (For interpretation of the references to colour in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the web version of this article.)

head.ce:title: Effect of gamma irradiation on carbon dot decorated polyethylene-gold@ hydroxyapatite biocomposite on titanium implanted repair for shoulder joint arthroplasty

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Shanghai Sixth People’s Hospital East Campus
Shanghai University of Medicine and Health

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: High disappointment rate of the ligament to hard tissue mending after the medical procedure has dependably been a testing issue in rotator cuff repair. Considering the elasticity of carbon dot decorated polyethylene (f-CDs-PE) and osteogenic movement of gold substituted hydroxyapatite (Au@HA) bioceramic, f-CDs-PE-Au@HA biocomposite coatings were created by an electrophoretic deposition method (EPD), the in vivo and in vitro bioactivity and cytocompatibility were researched. The physico-chemical properties of f-CDs-PE-Au@HA biocomposite coatings were characterized using fourier transform infra-red (FTIR) and X-Ray diffractometery (XRD). The morphology of the fabricated biocomposites was analyses via scanning electron microscopy (SEM) and transmission electron microscopy (TEM) methods. With a gamma-irradiation of f-CDs-PE-Au@HA biocomposite coating (BC2), the bond and multiplication of cells on biocomposite coating were improved. The specimen with a f-CDs-PE-Au@HA biocomposite (BC2) demonstrated a most noteworthy alkaline phosphatase activity articulation. The animal model consequences additionally show that the f-CDs-PE-Au@HA biocomposite (BC2) had great bioactive and cytocompatibility, which could develop the association of collagen and the arrangement of ligament and hard tissue. Expansion of the gamma-ray irradiation with f-CDs-PE-Au@HA biocomposite coating (BC2) at the tendon- hard tissue crossing point was exhibited to reinforce the mending entheses, increment hard tissue and tendon development and progress collagen association contrasted and control. The above outcomes have recommended that the progressive, implantable and solid stringy platforms built utilizing EPD extraordinary potential for enlargement of rotator cuff tears-recuperating.

ce:section.ce:para: Rotator cuff tear is a typical illness in the old, which may prompt shoulder agony and handicap [1]. Rotator cuff tear frequently happens at the ligament-bone inclusion site [2]. Ligament bone inclusion site is a fibro-cartilaginous progress region between bone and ligament, which provides to limit pressure focus at the intersection between hard tissue (bone) and delicate tissue (ligament) and to help the correspondence among numerous cell types to keep up interface capacity and homeostasis [3,4]. Considering the superb osseointegration and osteoinductivity of bioactive ceramics, they have pulled in an ever-increasing number of considerations in the field of ligament to bone recuperating [5,6]. Bio-ceramic including hydroxyapatite (HA) has been acquainted with improve the ligament to bone recuperating [7,8]. To advance the physiological attraction to the delicate tissue in tendon reproduction, HA with collagen composite was additionally infused into the inclusion site [9]. In past investigations with respect to ligament-bone recuperating upgrade, apatite has been shown to have beneficial outcomes in foremost cruciate tendon recreation models and rotator cuff tear fix models [10–12]. Conversely, assortments of biocomposite materials have been investigated with encouraging outcomes for ligament to hard tissue recuperating. Han et al. and, Kolluru et al. announced a ceramic-polymer biocomposite as a scaffold for both un-mineralized and mineralized tissues [13,14].

Polyethylene is one of the designing thermoplastics that have astounding mechanical and cytocompatibility properties [15]. Its items, for example, HDPE and polyethylene (PE), have been utilized as bearing parts in absolute joint substitutions because of their incredible properties [16]. It has been accounted for that PE can animate and improve the mending of the ligament to hard tissue fix to some degree [17]. PE composites consolidated with apatite have likewise been seriously investigated for hard tissue engineering since it can join the upsides of PE and bioceramics [18]. For the above reasons, it is normal that f-CDs-PE-Au@HA composite coatings can fill in as an incredible “connects” to interface hard tissues and ligament and advance the recuperating of the ligament to hard tissue addition. Also, PE could give a delicate lattice to ligament cells to join and develop.

In case of load bearing inserts like hard tissues applications, the inserts require essential solidness for ensuing osteogenesis with the encompassing bone tissue [19]. The procedure requires solid structural obsession of the implant in the encompassing articulating hard tissues. The covering exterior is frequently scratched and rubbed by encompassing articulating hard tissues amid the procedure [20]. This biodegradation procedure of the film can be s superlatively mimicked with reasonable scratch obstruction investigating progression. Additional, in the long haul in vivo utilization, ceaseless stacking and emptying cycle for orthopedic inserts likewise cause the consistent scraped area of the inserts exterior. This may brings about deposition disappointment and thus prompts antagonistic responses inside the body and consequently disappointment of the inserts. Additional, it is required to clean all artificial embeds ideally with-beam with an estimated radiation dose of 25 kGy [21–25]. This γ-light is perfect, advantageous, and compelling techniques for the cleansing of embed substances, which dispenses with the requirement for any unique bundling. The previous studies, researchers have made a clinical report and some detailed investigations on artificial joint implantation by applied with γ-irradiated composited biomaterials have been demonstrated that improved wear resistance properties comparable to the available conventional materials, in the presence and absence of accelerated aging. The alternative changes in the properties of crystalline structure, surface morphology had been found in the higher doses (30–150 kGy) of gamma irradiation for titanium-based alloys. Nevertheless, it was not observed in the low dose of gamma radiation, which is exhibited that lesser doses were insignificant. From the literature, the gamma irradiation doses at about 25 kGy (rate of 9.54 kGy/h) have been improved the bonding strength abilities of the bio-demineralized bone matrix composited materials. The impact of γ- light on the natural properties of interface/coating has been examined in detail especially on polymeric composites [26–30].

In this report, we have been prepared a novel nanoformulated f-CDs-PE-Au@HA composite through gamma irradiation for the first time. And also it is the very first time to provide detailed biological analysis of dual carbon dot and Au nanoparticles loaded nanocomposite for the bone implant applications. The reason for the present investigation was to assess whether the intervention of f-CDs-PE-Au@HA composite could improve the in vitro and in vivo properties in a rodent rotator cuff tear renovate representation. The researcher theorized that a) the mediation of f-CDs-PE-Au@HA composite amid rotator cuff tear fix would upgrade the ligament- hard tissue site mending; and a) f-CDs-PE-Au@HA composite would advance ligaments and tendons arrangement, and improve the recuperating structure in the interface region. In general, the current investigations conjectured that impact of γ-illumination on the f-CDs-PE-Au@HA composite coating was assessed by in vitro & in vivo test and contrasted with that of pure f-CDs-PE-Au@HA composite covering and exhibit a superior advancing impact in the interface territory.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fabrication of f-CDs, Au@HA, and f-CDs-PE-Au@HA Composite
ce:section.ce:para: f-CDs were fabricated by reference to a writing strategy [26]. Initially, 20 cm3 of Au solution was warmed to RT under vivacious blending. The pH of the arrangement was acclimated to 8 with sodium hydroxide, trailed by the expansion of 500 mg of apatite and blending was proceeded for 1 day, further, the suspension was cooled to RT.

The f-CDs, polyethylene (PE), and Au@HA nanoparticles were blended independently in weight ratios of 1:1:1 to acquire the required composites pre-mix. The mix was aggravated in a co-turning intermeshing twin screw extruder. The twin screw rate was kept steady at 15 rpm while the temperature of the extruder was kept consistent at about 200 °C. The expelled were breezed in a water shower and afterward slash into small pieces.

In particular, the blend of f-CDs-PE-Au@HA composite (0.6 g) were included into 60 mL isopropyl alcohol in 50 mL beaker and sonicated for 1 h to guarantee its homogeneous circulation. The pH estimations of the electrolyte were changed in accordance with 4. Before deposition, the electrolytes were refreshed for 3 h to permit suspension harmony and to guarantee the sedimentation of the huge or agglomerated particles. The EPD was directed in a 50 mL beaker at a consistent voltage of 50 V and coating times of 3 min, with a platinum electrode (anode) and titanium (cathode) 2 cm separated. Blending was utilized to keep up the homogeneous scattering of the particles amid the entire deposition progression. Subsequently, the deposited specimen were expelled cautiously from the electrolyte and dried at RT for 1 day. Following coating deposition, the biocomposite (BC2) were sterilized by illumination in a fixed compartment past a 60Co gamma-ray source (1 kGy every hour). One non-irradiation biocomposite (BC1) was kept for examination.

Surface morphology of the fabricated biocomposites was watched utilizing SEM (JEOL 5400) and TEM (JEOL-4000). The XRD pattern of the fabricated biocomposites was resolved with XRD-7000S. FT-IR was finished with Perkin Elmer 1600.

The osteoblast cells were brooded in cell culture plates at 3 × 104 cells/well in 96 culture plate and hatched for 1 day to take into consideration cell connection. The cell culture solution was traded with the extraction mode of the substance. Following brooding in a humidified climate for 1, 3 and 7 days, the refined cells were recolored with precious stone violet to take into account perceptions of the cell structure utilizing optical microscopy. Cell viability was resolved to utilizing a standard water-solvent tetrazolium salt as a cell expansion test for colorimetric examinations. As the osteoblast cell expansion explore, tests were put into a 96-well and 1 mL osteoblast medium was included in each culture well. To assess the basic alkaline phosphatase (ALP), cell densities of 3.0 × 104 cell/mL were utilized for 3- and 14-days examinations, separately. For the quantitative test, the cells were confined from the example surface and further hatched with pNPP at RT.

Sirius-red recoloring was utilized to decide the collagen emission on the biocomposites. Cell culture methodology is equivalent to that of the ECM explore. At each day, the biocomposites was expelled to another 96-culture well, washed two times with phosphate buffered saline and fixed in polyformaldehyde medium for 60 min. Subsequently, the biocomposite were doused into Sirius-Red arrangement and recolored at for 1 day. Lastly, the biocomposite were washed by acid and air desiccated before the pictures were taken. To get the quantitative examine, the stain was broken down by sodium hydroxide arrangement and the optical thickness of the absorbance at 540 nm was estimated.

The in vivo investigations were approved and carried out regarding the guidelines of our Institutional Ethical Committee (Shanghai Jiao Tong University, China) on the protection of animals used for scientific purposes. At each time point after the medical procedure, six rats for every gathering were apportioned for tomography examination. The recently framed hard tissue and thickness on the more noteworthy nodule were assessed with miniaturized scale CT. Subsequently thresholding, 3-D pictures were reproduced. The bone mineral thickness was determined over the ROI. For in vivo perception, the supraspinatus ligament humerus edifices were attached, got dried out, decalcified, and installed in paraffin. Areas with a thickness of 3 μm were cut in the fixed supraspinatus ligament bearing. The tissue areas were recolored with H&E and Sirius red. The pictures of these segments recolored with hematoxylin and eosin were taken under a magnifying lens. The pictures of tissue areas were recolored with picrosirius red could be caught with enraptured light microscopy for semi-quantitatively breaking down the collagen testimony and development degree at the association site of the ligament to the bone, after the system detailed in past writing [26].

The weedy peak at 3555 cm−1 was credited to the nearness of hydroxyl functional group from Au@HA. It was prominent that the peaks of phosphate frequency at 1106, 1029, 958, 611, 561 cm−1came about because of the PO4 3 gathering and the band of OH assemble appeared in the f-CDs-PE-Au@HA, 3538 considering the materials of Au@HA and f-CDs-PE [28]. While the intensity of bands from phosphate groups for both f-CDs-PE-Au@HA expanded with Au@HA amount [29].

XRD investigations were performed on fabricated biocomposite for Au@HA and f-CDs-PE-Au@HA (Fig. 1A). The diffraction planes fabricated materials, coordinated great with those of conventional apatite (PDF # 09-4032) at planes of 004, 213, 222, 300, 211, and 002 which were recorded to 2 theta 53, 49, 39, 32, 31, and 25, individually [30], demonstrating the development of apatite phase. The diffraction planes of the f-CDs-PE-Au@HA composite consequence to be the aggregate of those of the uncovered f-CDs-PE and the Au@HA. Besides, the previous pattern holds all the fundamental planes there in the JCPDS reference document for apatite [31].

Morphological transforms of f-CDs-PE-Au@HA biocomposite by gamma beam are analyzed utilizing SEM Fig. 2 . The SEM pictures of control (without gamma beam treated BC1) demonstrated long packs fibril morphology with critical interconnection framing a 3-D system (Fig. 2A, C) [32]. Though, gamma beam-treated f-CDs-PE-Au@HA (BC2) biocomposite increased interconnection between bundles of filaments and a level porous-like morphology expanded. Cross-area SEM was led to assess the thickness of the depositions (Fig. 2C, D). The normal thickness of the specimens BC1 and BC2, separately, are 1.5 μm and 2.5 μm. Emphasize that every one of the depositions was connected utilizing the equivalent lifting velocity and arrangements focus, which implies that the gamma beam is in charge of the improvement of the deposition thickness in specimen BC2 [33]. The formation of flower-like surface structure seems in the titanium implant surface which increases the coating strength with addition of γ-irradiation comparable than coating strength of biocomposite in the absence of radiation. Fig. 3A showcases a characteristic TEM picture of fabricated f-CDs, demonstrating a moderately indistinguishable size conveyance between 2.5 and 6.1 nm and the normal parallel measurement is around 3 nm as per the factual figuring of in excess of 150 specks. Development of interconnected permeable morphology is likewise affirmed from TEM pictures in Fig. 3B. It is obvious from Fig. 3B that, after gamma-ray, permeable 3D morphology is shaped [34, 35]. Additionally, impeccable biocomposite of the crystalline structure was accomplished by gamma-ray instigated, as appeared in Fig. 3C. Gamma-ray incited biocomposite (BC2) is another way to deal with incorporates any implants. Our new way to deal with getting permeable systems of BC2 biocomposite will be valuable for orthopedics originators and coordination of bio-gadgets. The coating adhesion scratch analysis results of gamma irradiated titanium surface with biocomposites were displayed in the Fig. 2 (E). The results of micro-scratch analysis exhibited that biocomposite coating with γ-irradiation has a greater enhancement of scratch adhesion comparable to the biocomposite coatings in the absence of γ-irradiation. This study clearly demonstrated that γ-irradiation has been favorable for the scratch adhesion and also be it is expected to boost up the cell proliferation on the implanted materials.

To analyze the impacts of osteoblast expansion, the biocomposite was eluted in the fitting medium, and the cells were developed for 1, 3 and 7 days. The cytoplasm and nuclei of the enduring cells were watched (Fig. 4A). Specifically, Fig. 4A demonstrates that just a couple of territories contained the cytoplasm, and there was a noteworthy reduction in the cell unit size and low cell development following 1 and 3 days. The gathering that was control and the pure biocomposite (BC1) demonstrated comparative cell development rates, while the gathering that was gamma-ray treated biocomposite (BC2) demonstrated the most astounding cell suitability. Following 7 days, the gamma-ray treated biocomposite (BC2) indicated cell development rates that were BC1. Though, the cells were as yet contracted contrasted with cells in the control gathering. The aftereffects of quantitative assessments of the cell viability rate utilizing the MTT measure (Fig. 4B) demonstrated that following 1 day, the gathering that was gamma-ray treated biocomposite (BC2) demonstrated the most elevated cell development, while the BC1 gathering had the least cell practicality. ECM mineralization was assessed by Alizarin Red recoloring and the outcomes appeared in Fig. 4C. It could be discovered that the recoloring results agreed well with the quantitative outcomes. Meanwhile, Extracellular matrix mineralization of the BC2 biocomposite gatherings was incredibly improved, and it expanded with the expansion of gamma-ray irradiation uncovering that Au@HA consolidation can animate ECM mineralization of osteoblast cell. Generally, osteoblast cells play a chief role in the bone tissues regeneration and also be provide rapid gowth factors of different proteins, including osteocalcin, osteopontin and particularly ALP, which effectively played in the bio-regulation of mineralization and attachment of osteoblasts to the bone implant materials. The results of cell survival analysis demonstrated that γ-irradiated biocomposites influences the biocompatibility and cell proliferation of osteoblast in the bone formation mechanism more significantly that does non-irradiated biocomposite [36, 37].

The alkaline phosphatase activities of the biocomposites following 7 and 14 days were resolved through a quantitative investigation (Fig. 5A, B). The control sample demonstrated the most reduced movement, while the gamma-ray treated biocomposite demonstrated the most noteworthy action. Additionally, after the gamma-ray treatments, the alkaline phosphatase activities expanded particularly, with the outcomes appearing same propensity as the cell multiplication following 7 days. Additionally, the Sirius-red recoloring results harmonized well with the quantitative outcomes (Fig. 5C). Contrasted with BC2 gathering, collagen formation was strikingly higher on biocomposite gatherings at each day. Likewise, collagen emission was essentially improved with the irradiation of gamma rays' content. Alkaline phosphatase (ALP) is extensively used as an osteoblast phenotypes marker, which is osteoblasts articulating the enzymes and greater amounts of ALP have been observed in the prepared biocomposite irradiated with γ-radiation. These findings specified that gamma-irradiated biocomposite influences the alteration of myoblasts into osteoblasts more proficiently than non-irradiated biocomposite.

The tomography pictures and bone mineral thickness of proximal humerus of all gatherings appeared in Fig. 6A. At about a month and a half after the medical procedure, BC2 and BC2 coatings still could be seen in the ligament to hard tissue site while just fresh tissue displayed in the control gathering. At 28 days after the medical procedure, for BC1 and BC2, the implanted coatings were undetectable from the tomography picture; rather, recently shaped hard tissue was obviously watched. The bone mineral density results demonstrated that bone mineral density estimations of the test bunches were critical bigger than the control aggregate, and the bone mineral density estimation of BC2 coating was the biggest among other gatherings (Fig. 6B, C).

H&E recolored tissue areas with the host tissue reaction and ligament development at each day appeared in Fig. 7A. Contrasted with the control gathering, more irritation cells and outside body response were seen in both test bunches at 14 and 28 days the medical procedure. In any case, at 14 days after medical procedure, no conspicuous irritation was seen in the test gatherings. It ought to be noticed that border tissues were routinely adjusted and cellularity diminished in the BC2 biocomposite at 28 days. The examination, fibrovascular granulation tissue of the control gathers recovered gradually at each day. The H&E recolored results had additionally affirmed that the great cytocompatibility of the BC2 biocomposite coating.

Affirming to the underlying aftereffects of H&E recoloring, the Sirius-Red recolored tissue segments demonstrated that collagen fiber packs of supraspinati ligament addition site of BC2 assemble adjusted more normally than BC1 and control gatherings (Fig. 7B). At about 14 and 28 days after the medical procedure, contrasted with the control gathering, test bunches showed a superior collagen association and type I collagen creation as indicated by the birefringence under spellbound light at the addition. At 28 days after the medical procedure, the exploratory gatherings demonstrated a fundamentally improved collagen association and type I collagen creation rather than the control gathering. Besides, the BC2 bunch displayed a vastly improved collagen association than BC1 gathering. The quantitative outcomes matched well with the recoloring pictures [38, 39].

In the current investigation, bioactive f-CDs-PE-Au@HA biocomposite coating was prepared via EPD strategy and investigated as a framework substance for the ligament to hard tissue recuperating. In vitro biocompatible assessments demonstrated that the BC2 biocomposite (gamma-ray treated) upgraded the grip and multiplication of osteoblast cell. Contrasted with the BC1 coating, the statement of alkaline phosphatase movement, collagen discharge, and ECM formation were likewise improved by the sterilizing using gamma rays (BC2). The animal model outcomes show that the f-CDs-PE-Au@HA biocomposite (BC2) coating had great cytocompatibility, which upgraded the arrangement of a designed ligament to the hard interface by civilizing the collagen association and the development of ligament and hard tissue. These progressive, biocompatible and implant frameworks engineered utilizing EPD might be a reasonable contender for the increase of rotator cuff tear-recuperating biomedical.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: The study was supported by the Projects of Science and Technology Development Foundation of Pudong New District, Shanghai, China (Grant Nos. PKJ2016-Y55 and PWZxq2017-03), and the study was also partly sponsored by the Interdisciplinary Program of Shanghai JiaoTong University (No. YG2017MS22, YG2017QN56 and YG2016QN22). The Program of Shanghai Sixth People's Hospital East Campus Foundation (NO. 2019YY001). The Program of Shanghai Sixth People's Hospital Foundation (NO. LY2Y0272). The Shanghai Three-year Action plan to Further Accelerate the Development of Traditional Chinese Medicine (2018–2020). No. ZY(2018-2020)-FWTX-8009.
